hero.title: Decoding the
hero.paragraph: Exploring the origins of galaxy formation and dark matter through large-scale computational simulations and high-energy observations.

about.title: A Passion for the Unseen Universe
paragraphs: My research focuses on the intersection of computational astrophysics and observational cosmology. Currently, I am a PhD student at Global Space University, where I investigate how dark matter halos influence the chemical enrichment of early galaxies.
Using the latest supercomputing clusters, I develop high-resolution simulations to mimic the first billion years of the universe.

focusItems.title: Large-scale Simulations
focusItems.desc: Running 10^7 particle simulations on the Aurora Supercomputer.

focusItems.title: Global Collaborations
focusItems.desc: Working with international teams across 4 continents.
